page: Amazon Web Services c8in.96xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by dedicated Intel processors running at 3.9 GHz. This instance features 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs via hyperthreading, managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-optimized allocation of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

The instance does not include local storage, requiring external EBS volumes for persistent data. However, it provides a high-capacity network baseline of 300 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network-intensive workloads and EBS data transfer. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.

Given its high core density and specialized 300 Gbps networking, the c8in.96xlarge offers resource density tailored for high-throughput network processing and compute-heavy tasks. The lack of local storage and GPUs represents a clear design tradeoff, focusing hardware resources entirely on raw x86 CPU execution and network bandwidth. This profile fits workloads such as network virtual appliances, compute-intensive distributed analytics, and high-performance web serving or batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8in.96xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel processors operating at 3.9 GHz. Managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 384 vCPUs across 192 physical cores and is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, optimizing it for high-throughput network operations and EBS-backed storage tasks. It contains no local storage and no GPUs, representing a design tradeoff that prioritizes high-density CPU resources and network performance over local disk I/O. This hardware profile is suited for compute-intensive workloads, network virtual appliances, and distributed data processing tasks that rely on external storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8in.96xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 384 Intel vCPUs, 768 GB of RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput network and compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8in.96xlarge is an Intel-powered, compute-optimized server with 384 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An Intel-powered, compute-optimized virtual server featuring three hundred gigabits per second network bandwidth and three hundred eighty-four virtual CPUs.
bullet_points: Powered by dedicated Intel processors running at 3.9 GHz
Features 384 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of system memory
Provides a high-capacity baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps
Utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor with zero local storage
Suited for network-intensive applications and compute-heavy batch processing
